title: microsorum cromwellii
intro: Microsorum cromwellii, also known as Polynesian peacock fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is traditionally used in Polynesian medicine for its various medicinal properties, including being used as a treatment for fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, contributing to its health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and aiding in wound healing. Microsorum cromwellii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to leverage its medicinal properties.
